# Copilot instructions — ai_assisting_code_learning

Purpose: give Copilot sessions quick, actionable context so suggestions and edits match repo workflows.

Build, test, and lint (explicit commands)

- macOS / Linux (Make):
  - Setup dev tools: make setup
  - Start dev server (hot reload): make dev
  - Build backend + frontend: make build
  - Run full test suite: make test
  - Run lint: make lint
  - Generate swagger docs: make swag
  - Run migrations: make migrate
  - Start dev containers: make docker-up

- Windows (PowerShell scripts):
  - Init: powershell -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File scripts/setup.ps1
  - Dev server: powershell -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File scripts/dev.ps1
  - Build: powershell -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File scripts/build.ps1

- Direct Go / Docker commands (examples):
  - Run server locally: go run ./cmd/server
  - Run a single package test: go test ./path/to/package -v
  - Run a specific test: go test ./path/to/package -run TestMyThing -v
  - Coverage: go test ./... -cover -coverprofile=coverage.out && go tool cover -html=coverage.out
  - Lint single package: golangci-lint run ./internal/yourpkg
  - Build docker image (deploy): docker build -f deployments/docker/Dockerfile -t ai-learning-platform .
  - Dev compose: podman compose -f docker-compose.dev.yml up -d  (Makefile also uses podman)

High-level architecture (big picture)

- Language & frameworks: Go 1.21 backend using Gin + GORM; frontend and docs live under web/ (VitePress + npm).
- Entrypoints:
  - Backend server: cmd/server (binary produced at bin/server when built)
  - DB migrations: cmd/migrate
  - API docs: generated by swag (router root: internal/app/handler/router.go)
- Code layout (important folders):
  - cmd/: application CLIs (server, migrate, etc.)
  - internal/: core application code (app/, biz/, data/, pkg/, server/)
  - configs/: YAML config files; configs/config.local.yaml is local override
  - deployments/: docker/k8s/terraform artifacts
  - web/: frontend & VitePress docs (web/docs/.vitepress/dist produced by CI)
  - tests/: categorized test suites (unit, integration, e2e, load)
- Infrastructure: Postgres, Redis, MinIO; Docker multi-stage build (Dockerfile) and CI job deploy-docs.yml publishes docs to gh-pages.
- Dev UX: Air (hot reload) is used in dev; Makefile bootstraps dev tools (installs air, golangci-lint, swag).

Key conventions and repo-specific patterns

- Use .env.local (copied from .env.example) and configs/config.local.yaml for local overrides — do not commit secrets.
- Windows-first scripts: repository provides PowerShell scripts in scripts/ for Windows dev flows; Makefile targets are the canonical commands on UNIX-like systems.
- Tests:
  - Running package-level tests is common: use go test ./path/to/package -v
  - Tests are grouped in tests/{unit,integration,e2e,load} for broader suites; CI and local runs may target different sets.
- Linting: golangci-lint is the linter; Makefile target make lint runs golangci-lint run ./...
- Swagger: API docs are generated with swag; handler router file is internal/app/handler/router.go (make swag runs swag init -g internal/app/handler/router.go -o docs/api).
- Migrations: make migrate runs go run cmd/migrate/main.go — follow migrations in deployments/postgres or migrations folder.
- Docker/Compose: Makefile uses podman compose for dev (docker-compose.dev.yml). CI and Dockerfile use multi-stage builds and put built binary at /app/server in container.
- Dev tools install: make setup downloads Go modules and installs dev tools (air, golangci-lint, swag).
- Binary & build outputs: backend binary target used in CI and deployment is bin/server locally, /app/server in Docker image.
- CI: .github/workflows/deploy-docs.yml builds docs (web) and publishes to gh-pages. Check workflows before editing CI behavior.

Where to look for deeper guidance

- Developer flow & setup: docs/SETUP_CHECKLIST.md
- Contribution & PR rules: docs/CONTRIBUTION_GUIDE.md
- Code review checklist: docs/CODE_REVIEW_GUIDE.md
- Git helper: GIT_GUIDE.md in repo root

Notes for Copilot sessions

- Prefer changes constrained to relevant packages; run make lint and make test locally (or suggest commands) before proposing large refactors.
- When adding/altering API endpoints, update Swagger via make swag and add integration tests under tests/.
- For Windows-specific flows, reference the PowerShell scripts in scripts/ rather than assuming POSIX shell availability.

(End of instructions)
